item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah yes the classic NK "End of Novelty" scenario.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Came back here for a re-read after hearing the news. RIP to the great Neil Peart, one of the greatest rock drummers to ever live.

This article works so well for me, as a Rush fan and frequent reader on the site. Great length, great tone, and a concept that isn't too on-the-nose while still feeling light and enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did I not know this existed. Rush is one of my favourite bands lmao.

+1! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, but the note at the end damn near killed it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate tribute bands so much, therefore, I love this. The addendum wasn't overly silly, it was like a perfect copy of my opinion translated to memo form. I would upvote twice if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, my bad. Didn't read it carefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing is, it's not a thing that can be locked in a box - it's an intangible phenomenon directly associated with a specific musical composition. The Foundation can't round up and destroy every existing recording of Caress of Steel because too much time has passed since when it was recorded and when the phenomenon manifested, they don't know what causes it to manifest on certain releases, and even if they bought out the record labels and made sure no new releases of it were ever made, there'd be an unknown number of affected discs still floating around in the wild (and possibly bootleg releases that could spread the phenomenon).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still kinda puzzled why it's Keter. It's true that if it escapes out, a massive chain reaction could begin and there'd be nothing but Rush. But as long as it's locked in a box and is not being played, nobody will get infected and therefore it can't do any harm unless it has some memetic effects that lure people into playing it. Kinda like 096, which could kill pretty much everybody if it's placed in the middle of New York City, but is leveled at Euclid.

Still, I like this SCP. +1

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I'm a big fan of what this article does, and I'm really glad to see that it's so well-rated, because it means I'm amongst people who share my particular buttons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hey, I should see what SCP-2112 is. That'd be really funny if it was a Rush reference."

"You've gotta be kidding me!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a singer who spends a lot of time in karaoke bars, I'm not sure that this isn't real. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely like this SCP, from the idea to the execution, but I notice in the comments that there's a bit of controversy about the addendum. My thoughts are as follows.

My thoughts
Password: -.-- -.-- --..

I do agree that no explanation is needed for classifying it Keter in its current form, but the addendum is still absolutely necessary. The last two sentences are the whole point of this article in my opinion. I think the focus should be shifted away from "highly contagious utter obsession likely resulting in the collapse of society" and put back on "business as usual, except art is Rush covers forever", which is far more chilling. The compulsion to drop everything, form a band, and spread the infection seems to have so great an effect on society that it starts to overshadow the real effect: the death of art.

I think if you power it down a little, it will work better. Instead of "going to any lengths" or "becoming obsessed with the idea", just have them be enthusiastically interested and go to reasonable lengths (definitely still have them expose others to SCP-2112 to make bandmates though). Here you can also add a disinterest in music not by Rush and art not on the cover of a Rush album, thereby reinforcing the real point. Maybe change "pandemic status within three weeks" to two months or so, no need to rush hurry it so much. My vision is that normal life should be able to go on without a hitch if this goes global ("but then why is it Keter?"), albeit at the terrible, terrible cost explained in the addendum ("oh… that's why.").


Shame on me for telling you how to fix an article that's already at+100, but I think powering it down will resolve the controversy over the addendum and reinforce the horror of Rush having "taken care of everything, the words you hear, the songs you sing, the pictures that give pleasure to your eye…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
read SCP number and title

open article, see image, instant upvote

wait what the fuck this text isn't green

Attention all sites of the SCP Foundation

Attention all sites of the SCP Foundation

Attention all sites of the SCP Foundation

WE HAVE ASSUMED CONTROL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After seeing that Series-3 had been opened up, I was hoping that -2112 was Rush related, and to my joy, it was. Definitely getting an upvote because I'm a huge music fan… though I would also be one susceptible to spreading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really didn't like this, but the ending addendum cemented the downvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I have "Closer To The Heart" stuck in my head like a wayward javelin. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a Canuck who grew up loving this band, the horror that could be unleashed makes me tremble inside.

Millions of cracked, warbling and off-key voices are now shrieking "Closer to the heart … " in my brain.

I hate you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unique concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, I despise that last Addendum. I already understood why this was Keter (it's nearly impossible to contain); I don't need some doctor smacking me in the face with the reasoning behind its classification.
Other than that, this SCP terrifies me. I can't imagine life without Gorillaz I wouldn't miss any other music the sheer variety of music society has created. Very tentative upvote; that Addendum is like a big zit on a supermodel's face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WGME?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still not sure about the Keter status, but I do like the weirdness in this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Count my vote for the addendum, it made a SCP which was bordering on a joke into something which when you think about it is quite a unique vision of hell in the Foundationverse.

Oh sure, we could all have our necks snapped by a plague of 173 but to have all art,culture and music reduced to the output of one band is quietly horrifying (with respect to fans of Rush!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda feel like this is a borderline SCP-J. Well written, but it seems too silly. I'm neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually a really interesting article. My only real complaint is that it won't really stick with anyone that isn't already a Rush fan. Still, it could be sort of hilarious to have people who don't know about Rush read this and then find out that they're real. Hats off to you, dear sir, and a +1 as a blessing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. It exemplifies that "Keter" means "extremely hard to contain", not "s00per ultra killy end 0f tha wurld".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the last note is pretty unnecessary. You've already stated in the article that people are willing to quit their jobs etc in order to do this, which would cause complete and utter financial collapse if it ever spread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, my problem with the addendum was that I read it as "the person writing the report is using it as an excuse to shrill their band" and not "the researcher is infected". I'm not sure how you could correct that though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at Rush.

Then you added the memetic properties. And I fell hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like SCP-785 but without the subtlety, horror, and originality that made that one a classic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If that's the part that people are complaining about (I think it is), then that seems pretty self-evident, yeah.

Granted, I thought it was okay with the researcher being infected, but it can probably stand without it and lose nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Which is why I wrote the addendum in the first place. Sigh.

Would it be better if I put back the first paragraph of the addendum and left out the part implying the researcher is infected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Without the addendum, I'm just going, "Why is this Keter?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually liked the addendum that explained how detrimental it could be to us other than our health, it was just the final part about the musical project that kinda ruined it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like a lot of people are on the fence about the addendum after all. I thought it worked pretty well as a stinger, but if it's coming off too OTT I'll remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Put me down as another person who likes everything but the addendum. It just feels too goofy and too unprofessional for a mainlister. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I should put up a barrier. You know, to keep myself intact and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum isn't doing it for me, but I liked the rest of the article just enough to +1. I thought it was an interesting concept/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr.1: So what are you doing today?
Dr.2 analyzing SCP-173 fecal matter, You?
Dr.1 Interviewing Neil Peart to see if he knows anything about the spread of SCP-2112
Dr.2 …..Trade?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …neutral vote for now, given the addendum is simply too silly to fit with the rest of the tone of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I don't really like the lack of subtlety in the addendum. I find it over-dramatic actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is proof that not every Keter has to be life-threatening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. It's kind of a novel concept, but there's nothing here to make me care after I'm finished.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…goddammit, Smapti. As a thirty-odd year fan of Rush, I'm torn. On the plus side, it's well-written, with references to the band's works interspersed smoothly in the text; Caress of Steel is probably the perfect choice as the vector; and the consequences of outbreak are, as you describe, horrific in their own right. On the other hand, the last bit of the addendum wrecked the gravitas you had going for the whole rest of the article, and, to be honest, choosing thematically-related SCP numbers tends to give me pause.

Then again, "2112" is about suppression of art and individuality, and the end results of both 2112 scenarios are eerily similar. So take my +1, Smapti— if there's gonna be a 2112 SCP, this needs to be it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is Series 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well done securing the very first non-contest Series 2 SCiP! It is well deserving of the spot. Had good tone, good writing, and I overall liked it.

Also, YOU WILL BE ASSIMILATED TO THE RUSH. THE RUSH IS YOUR MASTER NOW. HEIL.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bugfuck hate the addendum at the end for all the reasons you expected people like me would hate it. Upvoted for everything else. It's like the soundtrack to SCP-785.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All hail 2112

All hail 2112

It is truly worthy of this position

It's perfect

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first official noncontest Series 3. And it's really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since Series 3 has now officially been opened, I hereby present an article that popped into my head several weeks ago based specifically around this number.
